2604.06838.title: Explaining Neural Networks in Preference Learning: a Post-hoc Inductive Logic Programming Approach
2604.06838.paper_summary: In this paper, we propose using Learning from Answer Sets to approximate black-box models, such as Neural Networks (NN), in the specific case of learning user preferences. We specifically explore the use of ILASP (Inductive Learning of Answer Set Programs) to approximate preference learning systems through weak constraints. We have created a dataset on user preferences over a set of recipes, which is used to train the NNs that we aim to approximate with ILASP. Our experiments investigate ILASP both as a global and a local approximator of the NNs. These experiments address the challenge of approximating NNs working on increasingly high-dimensional feature spaces while achieving appropriate fidelity on the target model and limiting the increase in computational time. To handle this challenge, we propose a preprocessing step that exploits Principal Component Analysis to reduce the dataset's dimensionality while keeping our explanations transparent. Under consideration for publication in Theory and Practice of Logic Programming (TPLP).
2604.06838.paper_summary_zh: 在本文中，我們提出使用從答案集學習來近似黑箱模型，例如神經網絡（NN），特別是在學習用戶偏好的情況下。我們特別探討使用 ILASP（答案集程序的歸納學習）通過弱約束來近似偏好學習系統。我們創建了一個關於用戶對一組食譜的偏好的數據集，該數據集用於訓練我們希望用 ILASP 近似的神經網絡。我們的實驗調查了 ILASP 作為神經網絡的全局和局部近似器。這些實驗解決了在越來越高維的特徵空間中近似神經網絡的挑戰，同時在目標模型上實現適當的保真度並限制計算時間的增加。為了應對這一挑戰，我們提出了一個預處理步驟，利用主成分分析來降低數據集的維度，同時保持我們的解釋透明。正在考慮發表於《邏輯編程的理論與實踐》（TPLP）。
2604.06838.author: Daniele Fossemò et.al.
2604.06838.authors: Daniele Fossemò, Filippo Mignosi, Giuseppe Placidi, Luca Raggioli, Matteo Spezialetti, Fabio Aurelio D'Asaro
